subject: internal compliance note — pdpl controls, kma disclosure timing, and transaction workflow for al noor renewable holdings jsc (cr 1010-abc-0000)
context: proposed strategic partnership with red dune foods co. (unlisted), and reference to sahra exchange notice dated 12 safar 1446h about exploratory discussions (public, stale)
pdpl reminder: do not collect or transmit national id/iqama numbers; use role-based ids only; placeholders below are invalid by design
form labels for vendor onboarding: national id/iqama: 0000-xxxx-0000; commercial registration: 1010-xyz-9999; iban: sa00 0000 0000 0000 0000 0000
ndad transfer note: any cross-border processing requires a transfer impact assessment and standard contractual clauses logged in the ndad portal before exporting pseudonymized data
mnpi screen: the investor-relations draft must not circulate outside the deal team before the embargo time of 15 shawwal 1447h 10:00 (ksa)
deal status: the share purchase agreement (spa) between al noor renewable holdings jsc and red dune foods co. has been executed; signing occurred after market close and is not yet published on sahra exchange
covenant update: no material adverse change has been alleged or triggered under the interim covenants as of this memo
disclosure controls: use only public facts until the kma filing and sahra exchange disclosure go live; avoid forward-looking percentages or amounts in drafts
records: redact direct identifiers from board packs; use hashed references rather than iqama or cr numbers; store the mapping table in a segregated drive
internal contacts: send questions to compliance@alnoor.sa and ir@alnoor.sa; do not cc external advisors until nda status is confirmed
risk clarifier: “spa day” for the wellness team next week is an hr initiative and unrelated to the “spa” defined above
project management: upload the diligence checklist with lowercase filenames, tag sources as public/source or private/internal, and annotate stale articles accordingly
reminder: this note provides operational guidance and does not alter obligations under the ksa pdpl, the kma market conduct rules, or the sahra exchange listing framework
